semanticCore: Withholding, restraining, telling different accounts, or concealing the truth

keyDerivations.meaning: It is not, there is not (negative particle implying lack of time/escape)

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَنَادَوْا وَلَاتَ حِينَ مَنَاصٍ (38:3): And they cried out, but it was not a time of escape.
discardedNoise: Al-Lat (pagan goddess name) - though spelled similarly, has a distinct historical context.
summaryEn: The root yield the negative particle Lat (is not/there is not), representing the eschatological reality where warning comes too late and no escape is possible.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر النفي والمنع والتخفي (لوت)، مستخدماً أداة النفي (لات) لتبيان فوات الأوان وضياع فرصة الفرار من العقوبة الإلهية.
rawText: اللَّاتُ : see اللَّاتُّ, in art. لت. لَاتَ 1 , aor. يَلُوتُ, He told, narrated, or gave an account of, a thing different from that respecting which he was asked. (K.) [But accord. to some, the aor. is يَلِيتُ, and the verb belongs to art. ليت.] It was said to ElAsadeeyeh, “ What is المُدَاحَلَةُ ” and she answered أَنْ يَلِيتَ الإِنْسَانُ شَيْئًا قَدْ عَلِمَهُ, i. e., “ The concealing a thing that one knows, and telling, or narrating, something different from it. ” (TA.) === لَاتَ الخَبَرَ, aor. يَلُوتُ, He concealed the news, or information, (K,) and related what was different therefrom. (TA.) [But see above.] === لَاتَ الرَّجُلَ, aor. يَلُوتُ, He told the man, or narrated to him, a thing in a manner different from the real state of the case: or he expressed the news, or information, to him obscurely, or enigmatically, or obscured it to him, or concealed it from him, telling him, narrating to him, or giving him an account of, a thing different from that respecting which he was asked: but accord. to As., لَاتَهُ, aor. يَلِيتُهُ, [not يَلُوتُهُ.] inf. n. لَيْتٌ, signifies “ he expressed to him the news, or information, obscurely, or enigmatically, or obscured it to him, or concealed it from him ”: thus he makes it belong to art. ليت: and the like is said in the L. See also above. (TA.) === لَاتَهُ, aor. يَلُوتُ; as also لَاتَهُ, aor. يَلِيتُ; He withheld him, or restrained him, and turned him, or averted him, from his course, purpose, or object. (S, K, art ليت, q. v.)
